summary: Phase B backend-only testing for GATE Study OS — Resource Study Notes + Important Pages. Wrote /app/backend/tests/test_resource_notes.py with 16 tests covering all requested scenarios. All 16/16 pytest tests passed (100%). No 5xx errors observed in backend logs.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 2424 lines — well past the 700-line guideline. The Phase B endpoints (notes/toggle/label) are good candidates to split into /app/backend/routers/resource_notes.py.

_normalize_pages truncation: label is clamped to 200 chars in toggle/label endpoints but NOT in save_resource_notes (POST /notes accepts arbitrarily long labels via the dict form). Consider centralizing the 200-char cap inside _normalize_pages for consistency.

POST /api/resources/{rid}/notes silently ignores unknown keys but also silently drops malformed important_pages entries (str/None inside the list are eaten by the try/except). Acceptable, but a 'rejected' count in the response would help client-side debugging.

save_resource_notes returns the merged doc — but if the caller sends content=None AND important_pages=None (empty body), an upsert still happens writing only updated_at. Low-risk noop write; could short-circuit when both fields are None.

DELETE cascade only clears db.resource_notes. If future collections (e.g., resource_highlights, resource_annotations) are added, they must be added to delete_resource — easy to miss. Consider a helper _purge_resource(rid, uid).
test_credentials: Minted via helper in /app/memory/test_credentials.md. PRIMARY_TOKEN=test_session_f7ba4b5587a04ae88b5ba46cac3165d0 (user_test_primary), SECONDARY_TOKEN=test_session_13cf10f4dc4b4ebab6e305f4044bb682 (user_test_secondary). Both valid 7 days. Both non-admin.
seed_data_creation: Each test creates its own TEST_-prefixed resource and deletes it in a finally block — no residual data. No global seed needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run with: PRIMARY_TOKEN=<tok> SECONDARY_TOKEN=<tok> pytest /app/backend/tests/test_resource_notes.py -v. Mint two fresh tokens via the python snippet in /app/memory/test_credentials.md (user_id 'user_test_primary' and a second one like 'user_test_secondary'). The existing /app/backend/tests/test_gate_os_backend.py (Phase A) still uses ADMIN_TOKEN/USER_TOKEN env names — independent test set.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A - all tests pass, no bugs found in Phase B endpoints.
